text.title: Code Decomposition Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules every decomposition pass must follow. The detector is a static check + a test re-run between moves.

rule.statement: Before any move, classify the file under one of the five patterns. No move list is valid without a stated pattern.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit pattern, 'decomposition' becomes ad-hoc relocation that creates fresh boundary debt.

rule.statement: Each move is followed by a test run; subsequent moves are blocked until tests are green.
rule.rationale: Compounding moves under a red suite cascades; isolating each move bisects the failing change immediately.

rule.statement: Use `git mv` (or equivalent) for file moves; never delete-and-create. Squash-merge is forbidden during the decomposition PR.
rule.rationale: Renames + content edits in the same commit hide the move from git log --follow; future blame becomes useless.

rule.statement: Each commit moves one symbol (or one tightly-cohesive cluster) and updates exactly its callers. No multi-symbol mega-commits.
rule.rationale: Per-symbol commits make rebase + revert trivial; mega-commits force re-doing the work.

rule.statement: The pre-decomposition public exports are re-exported from the original file location until a separate API-migration PR removes them.

rationale.source: Internal — observed in mediamanager monorepo Q4 2025

rule.rationale: Decomposition + API break in one PR is the most common cause of broken downstream consumers.
